text:
Market today: The mood is mixed, with rising oil prices creating general market jitters, but the real driver for your book is chip sector health. Samsung's recent report points to demand concerns, which directly impacts your concentrated tech/chip holdings.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern, sitting at a -23.52% loss with a 'SELL' status. SPAL is a new, leveraged position already down -11.17%. Remember, trading on margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on any further news regarding chip demand, especially after Samsung's report. Also, monitor GOOGL for follow-through on its nuclear fusion news.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing your leveraged, underperforming positions and stay vigilant on chip sector sentiment.
plays: Trim SOXL: It's a leveraged 'SELL' down -23.52%. Respect its stop at 200 to manage risk on this volatile position.
Monitor SPAL: This new, leveraged position is down -11.17%. Keep its stop at 21.38 firm to limit further drawdowns.
Consider LRCX: Scanner shows a 'BUY' for this chip name (entry 349.85-349.23, stop 347.77), offering a potential long entry in a discounted area.
